description: prd-gen CLI Tool - Single-story-at-a-time PRD review with priority assignment, editing, add/delete; changes saved to prd.json

userStories.title: Project scaffold and npm package setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a runnable npm package so that users can install and run prd-gen via npx.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with name 'prd-gen', bin entry pointing to cli entry point
Entry point file exists and is executable
Running `node index.js` (or equivalent) does not throw on startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: HTTP server that loads prd.json and serves frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a native http server that reads prd.json and serves a static HTML page so that the browser UI can display stories.
acceptanceCriteria: Server starts on a local port using Node's native `http` module (no Express)
GET / serves an HTML page
GET /api/stories returns JSON array from ./prd.json (empty array if file missing)
POST /api/stories accepts JSON body and writes to ./prd.json
Browser is opened automatically on start using `open` package or child_process
Ctrl+C shuts down gracefully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Basic story card UI with title, description and story counter
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current story's title and description in a centered card so that I can review it.
acceptanceCriteria: Page shows a centered container (~800px wide) with a soft shadow on white (#FFFFFF) background
Current story title shown in ~48px bold black (#000)
Current story description shown in ~20px #333, multiline
Top-right corner shows 'Story X / Y' in grey small text
If prd.json is empty or missing, page shows 'No stories to review.' with an Add button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Priority badge and 1–10 priority selector buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current priority and set a new one by clicking numbered buttons so that I can assign priority quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Priority badge (#FF2D55 circle) shown on the story card displaying current priority (1–10) or empty if unset
10 round buttons labelled 1–10 shown at the bottom of the card
Selected priority button is highlighted in #FF2D55; others are grey
Hovering a button turns it #FF2D55
Clicking a button sets that priority and marks the form as dirty
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inline editing for title and description
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the title or description to edit them inline so that I can correct or improve story text.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the title makes it an editable input/contenteditable field
Clicking the description makes it an editable textarea/contenteditable field
Editing either field marks the form as dirty
Pressing Esc while a field is focused blurs it without reverting changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Save button and Next story navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Save button when changes are unsaved and a Next story button when clean so that I can progress through the review.
acceptanceCriteria: When form is clean, bottom-center shows a grey 'Next story' button
When form is dirty, bottom-center shows a red (#FF2D55 with white text) 'Save' button
Clicking Save POSTs changes to /api/stories and writes prd.json; shows thin red spinner during write
After save, button returns to 'Next story'
Clicking 'Next story' advances to the next story
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fanned story stack behind current card
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the remaining stories stacked behind the current card so that I can get a sense of how many are left.
acceptanceCriteria: Stories behind current card are shown as fanned cards
1–5 remaining: all cards visible in the fan
6–15 remaining: top 5 cards visible + '+N' label
16+ remaining: top 3 cards visible + '+N' label
Fan shrinks as Next is clicked (card count decreases)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add new story button
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add a new story before the current one so that I can capture ideas during review.
acceptanceCriteria: A '+' icon appears at the top-left of the card on hover
Clicking '+' inserts a new blank story immediately before the current story with defaults: title 'New Item', description 'Describe here…', priority null
After save, view returns to the original story position
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Delete story with confirmation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete a story with a confirmation prompt so that I don't accidentally remove stories.
acceptanceCriteria: A red trash icon appears at the top-right of the card on hover
Clicking trash shows a confirmation dialog with text 'Delete this story? No undo.'
Confirming deletes the story and saves prd.json immediately
Cancelling closes the dialog without changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Keyboard shortcuts
userStories.description: As a power user, I want keyboard shortcuts for all actions so that I can review stories without touching the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Keys 1–9 set priority 1–9 globally (even when not editing text)
Key 0 sets priority 10 globally
Enter or Space triggers Save when dirty, or Next story when clean
Backspace or Delete opens the delete confirmation dialog
N inserts a new story (same as clicking '+')
Esc closes any open confirmation dialog, or blurs the currently focused edit field
Priority keys are ignored when a text input/textarea is focused
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: End-of-review summary screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a summary after reviewing all stories so that I know the session is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: After the last story, 'Next story' button becomes a dimmed 'Finish' button
Clicking Finish shows a summary screen with: 'Good job! You've reviewed all stories.' and counts for Reviewed, Created (new), Deleted
An 'Add new story' button is still visible on the summary screen and appends to the end
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
